sentence: O ceo non está claro hoxe.
ipa: o ˈθeo ˈnoŋ eˈsta ˈklaɾo ˈoʃe
gloss_en: The sky is not clear today.
notes: Distinción ⟨c⟩ [θ] 'ceo' (general in the Navia-Eo zone); word-final velar [ŋ] 'non' per frias_conde2005.

sentence: Estos zapatos custan corenta euros.
ipa: ˈestos θaˈpatos ˈkustaŋ koˈɾenta ˈewɾos
gloss_en: These shoes cost forty euros.
notes: ⟨z⟩ [θ] 'zapatos'; regional ⟨estos⟩; coda [ŋ] 'custan'; ⟨s⟩+voiced sandhi per alla_normas_gallego_asturiano2007.

sentence: A chegada dos amigos é mañá ás tres.
ipa: a tʃeˈɣaða dos aˈmiɣos ˈɛ maˈɲa ˈas ˈtɾes
gloss_en: The friends' arrival is tomorrow at three.
notes: Word-initial ⟨ch⟩ [tʃ] robustly preserved 'chegada' [tʃeɣaða]; open-mid [ɛ] 'é'; palatal nasal ⟨ñ⟩ [ɲ] 'mañá' per frias_conde2005.

sentence: Meu irmán vive preto da aldea.
ipa: ˈmew iˈɾmaŋ ˈbibe ˈpɾeto da ˈaldea
gloss_en: My brother lives near the village.
notes: Word-final velar [ŋ] 'irmán'; regional 'aldea'; intervocalic /ɡ/ lenited [ɣ] carried from the Galician base per alla_normas_gallego_asturiano2007.

sentence: Vai de fronte e torce pola primeira congostra.
ipa: ˈbaj de ˈfɾonte e ˈtoɾθe ˈpɔla pɾiˈmejɾa koˈnɡostɾa
gloss_en: Go straight ahead and turn down the first narrow path.
notes: No Ĕ/Ŏ diphthongisation 'fronte'/'torce' (Galaico-Portuguese, not Asturleonese); regional 'congostra' (narrow path); distinción 'torce' [θ] per frias_conde2005.

sentence: A caldeirada daquel sitio estaba moi boa.
ipa: a kaldeˈjɾaða daˈkel ˈsitjo eˈstaβa ˈmoj ˈboa
gloss_en: The fish stew at that place was very good.
notes: Regional 'caldeirada'; the transitional variety keeps the Galician hiatus/sandhi behaviour per fernandez_rei1990.

sentence: Onde está a chave da corte?
ipa: ˈonde eˈsta a ˈtʃabe da ˈkoɾte
gloss_en: Where is the cowshed key?
notes: Affricate ⟨ch⟩ [tʃ] 'chave' preserved word-initially; regional 'corte' (cowshed) per frias_conde2005.

sentence: Cantas persoas veñen cear?
ipa: ˈkantas ˈpeɾsoas ˈbeɲeŋ ˈθeaɾ
gloss_en: How many people are coming to dinner?
notes: Palatal nasal 'veñen' [ɲ] with a velar coda [ŋ]; distinción ⟨ce⟩ [θ] 'cear' per frias_conde2005.

sentence: Se eu puidese, mercaría aquela casa vella.
ipa: se ˈew pwiˈðese meɾkaˈɾia aˈkela ˈkasa ˈbeʎa
gloss_en: If I could, I would buy that old house.
notes: Regional 'mercar'; palatal lateral ⟨ll⟩ [ʎ] 'vella' retained (no yeísmo) per alla_normas_gallego_asturiano2007.

sentence: Onte merquei cinco quilos de castañas na feira.
ipa: ˈonte ˈmeɾkej ˈθinko ˈkilos de kaˈstaɲas na ˈfejɾa
gloss_en: Yesterday I bought five kilos of chestnuts at the fair.
notes: Distinción ⟨c⟩ [θ] 'cinco'; 'castañas' [ɲ]; ⟨s⟩+voiced sandhi 'castañas na' per frias_conde2005.

sentence: O ano que vén hei viaxar a Navia.
ipa: o ˈano ke ˈbɛŋ ˈej bjaˈʃaɾ a ˈnabja
gloss_en: Next year I will travel to Navia.
notes: Open-mid [ɛ] 'vén' [bɛŋ]; ⟨x⟩ [ʃ] 'viaxar'; place-name Navia (the eastern river border) per fernandez_rei1990.

sentence: Chámame cando chegues á casa.
ipa: ˈtʃamame ˈkando ˈtʃeɡes ˈa ˈkasa
gloss_en: Call me when you get home.
notes: Affricate ⟨ch⟩ [tʃ] 'chámame'/'chegues' preserved per frias_conde2005.

sentence: Boas tardes, como che vai a vida?
ipa: ˈboas ˈtaɾdes ˈkomo tʃe ˈbaj a ˈbiða
gloss_en: Good afternoon, how is life treating you?
notes: Clitic ⟨ch⟩ [tʃ] 'che'; hiatus 'vai a' per fernandez_rei1990.

sentence: Máis vale paxaro na man que cento voando.
ipa: ˈmais ˈbale paˈʃaɾo na ˈmaŋ ke ˈθento ˈboando
gloss_en: A bird in the hand is worth two in the bush.
notes: ⟨x⟩ [ʃ] 'paxaro'; distinción ⟨ce⟩ [θ] 'cento'; word-final velar [ŋ] 'man' per frias_conde2005.

sentence: O can corre pola ribeira do río.
ipa: o ˈkaŋ ˈkɔre ˈpɔla riˈβejɾa do ˈrio
gloss_en: The dog runs along the riverbank.
notes: Word-final velar [ŋ] 'can'; trill [r] 'corre'/'río' vs tap 'ribeira' per fernandez_rei1990.

sentence: A muller vella colle a agulla amarela.
ipa: a muˈʎeɾ ˈbeʎa ˈkoʎe a ˈaɡʎa amaˈɾela
gloss_en: The old woman picks up the yellow needle.
notes: Palatal lateral ⟨ll⟩ [ʎ] 'muller'/'vella'/'colle'/'agulla' — /ʎ/ retained per alla_normas_gallego_asturiano2007.

sentence: A chuvia cae sobre o teito de lousa.
ipa: a ˈtʃubja ˈkae ˈsobɾe o ˈtejto de ˈlowsa
gloss_en: The rain falls on the slate roof.
notes: Word-initial ⟨ch⟩ [tʃ] 'chuvia' [tʃubja] and the -CT- outcome ⟨teito⟩ [tejto] (Galaico-Portuguese [it], not Castilian [tʃ]); falling diphthong [ej] per frias_conde2005.

sentence: A viña da montaña dá bo viño.
ipa: a ˈbiɲa da moˈntaɲa ˈda ˈbo ˈbiɲo
gloss_en: The vineyard on the mountain gives good wine.
notes: Palatal nasal ⟨ñ⟩ [ɲ] 'viña'/'montaña'/'viño' per frias_conde2005.

sentence: Unha das mulleres colleu algunha flor.
ipa: uŋa das muˈʎeɾes ˈkoʎew ˈalɡŋa ˈfloɾ
gloss_en: One of the women picked some flower.
notes: Palatal lateral ⟨ll⟩ [ʎ] 'mulleres'/'colleu'; velar nasal ⟨nh⟩ [ŋ] 'unha'/'algunha' per alla_normas_gallego_asturiano2007.

sentence: A ponte da vila ten doce arcos de pedra.
ipa: a ˈponte da ˈbila ˈteŋ ˈdoθe ˈaɾkos de ˈpedɾa
gloss_en: The town bridge has twelve arches of stone.
notes: Distinción ⟨ce⟩ [θ] 'doce'; word-final velar [ŋ] 'ten'; regional 'ponte' per frias_conde2005.
